title: Contributing to pft
text: Particularly welcome: Bug reports, especially minimal reproducible example (sex, age, height, race + expected vs. actual output). Documentation improvements — typos, clearer wording, additional examples. Tests close gaps ’ve found. Cross-validation results implementations (rspiro, GLI web calculator, vendor software) — particularly ’ve found discrepancy. Less likely accepted without discussion first (open issue): New reference equations outside GLI family (NHANES III, JRS, Knudson, Crapo, etc.). pft’s scope GLI / ERS-ATS 2022 framework; equations belong rspiro similar packages. Interpretation algorithms outside Stanojevic 2022 framework. Vendor file-format readers — variety huge scope drift substantial.

title: Contributing to pft
text: Fork repository create branch main. Install dev dependencies renv::restore() project root. Make change, tests applicable. Run R CMD check ---cran ensure new findings. Run test suite devtools::test() via R CMD check. Update NEWS.md one-line bullet describing change. Open pull request referencing related issue including brief rationale.

title: Diffusion capacity (DLCO / TLCO): reference, Hb correction, interpretation
text: pft_diffusion() implements GLI 2017 standard (Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2017, 2020 author correction applied) adults children aged 5-90 years (GLI calculator caps 85; underlying spline tables extend 90). default emits traditional units (DLCO, KCO, VA mL/min/mmHg, mL/min/mmHg/L, L respectively); SI.units = TRUE switches SI units (TLCO KCO mmol/min/kPa mmol/min/kPa/L). VA column either way. output carries per-measure _pred / _lln / _uln / _zscore / _pctpred shape pft_spirometry() pft_volumes().
code: patient <- data.frame(   sex = "M", age = 45, height = 178,   dlco_measured   = 22.0,   va_measured     = 5.8,   kco_tr_measured = 3.79 ) out <- pft_diffusion(patient) out[, grep("dlco|va|kco", colnames(out), value = TRUE)] #> # A tibble: 1 × 18 #>   dlco_measured va_measured kco_tr_measured dlco_pred dlco_lln dlco_uln #>           <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1            22         5.8            3.79      30.3     23.4     38.3 #> # ℹ 12 more variables: dlco_zscore <dbl>, dlco_pctpred <dbl>, #> #   kco_tr_pred <dbl>, kco_tr_lln <dbl>, kco_tr_uln <dbl>, kco_tr_zscore <dbl>, #> #   kco_tr_pctpred <dbl>, va_pred <dbl>, va_lln <dbl>, va_uln <dbl>, #> #   va_zscore <dbl>, va_pctpred <dbl>

title: Diffusion capacity (DLCO / TLCO): reference, Hb correction, interpretation
text: DLCO measured standard reference Hb may misrepresent patients anemic (DLCO -reads) polycythemic (DLCO -reads). pft_dlco_hb_correct() applies Cotes 1972 formula express measured DLCO standard reference Hb: DLCOadj=DLCO⋅1.7⋅HbrefHb+0.7⋅Hbref. \text{DLCO}_\text{adj} =   \text{DLCO} \cdot \frac{1.7 \cdot \text{Hb}_\text{ref}}{\text{Hb} + 0.7 \cdot \text{Hb}_\text{ref}}. reference Hb age- sex-dependent: 146 g/L males aged >= 15, 134 g/L females children < 15 either sex (Cotes 1972 / Stanojevic 2017 Table 5). Pass hemoglobin g/L (package detect convert g/dL inputs). Apply correction computing z-scores comparing across patients whose Hb varies; GLI 2017 reference values assume Hb sex-/age-specific standard.
code: # Anemic adult male: corrected DLCO is higher than measured. pft_dlco_hb_correct(dlco = 20.0, hemoglobin = 110, sex = "M", age = 45) #> [1] 23.39303  # Polycythemic adult male: corrected is lower than measured. pft_dlco_hb_correct(dlco = 25.0, hemoglobin = 180, sex = "M", age = 45) #> [1] 21.98795

title: Diffusion capacity (DLCO / TLCO): reference, Hb correction, interpretation
text: pft_diffusion_interpret() classifies diffusion result one six clinical categories per Hughes & Pride 2012 framework (adopted Stanojevic 2017 task force). classifier uses z-scores , works identically traditional SI columns: decision tree (Stanojevic 2017 / Hughes & Pride 2012): Categories label z-score pattern . Hughes & Pride 2012 describes differential diagnosis associated pattern; interpretation scope package. pft_interpret() classifier runs automatically whenever diffusion z-score columns present, diffusion_category column attached free standard workflow:
code: mixed_cohort <- data.frame(   dlco_zscore   = c(-0.5, -2.0, -2.5, -2.5, -2.0,  0.0),   va_zscore     = c(-0.5, -0.5, -2.0, -2.5, -0.5,  0.0),   kco_tr_zscore = c(-0.5, -2.0,  0.0, -2.5,  0.5,  2.0) ) pft_diffusion_interpret(mixed_cohort) #>   dlco_zscore va_zscore kco_tr_zscore   diffusion_category #> 1        -0.5      -0.5          -0.5               Normal #> 2        -2.0      -0.5          -2.0          Parenchymal #> 3        -2.5      -2.0           0.0          Volume loss #> 4        -2.5      -2.5          -2.5                Mixed #> 5        -2.0      -0.5           0.5 Vascular (suggested) #> 6         0.0       0.0           2.0         Elevated KCO patient2 <- data.frame(   sex = "F", age = 60, height = 165, race = "Caucasian",   fev1_measured    = 1.6, fvc_measured    = 1.9,   fev1fvc_measured = 0.84, tlc_measured   = 4.0,   dlco_measured   = 10.0, va_measured    = 3.5,   kco_tr_measured = 2.86 ) r <- pft_interpret(patient2) r[, c("ats_classification", "diffusion_category")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   ats_classification diffusion_category #>   <chr>              <chr>              #> 1 Restricted         Mixed

what: 4. How VA shapes the classifier output
title: Diffusion capacity (DLCO / TLCO): reference, Hb correction, interpretation
text: Alveolar volume (VA) axis splits restriction classifier’s two volume-loss categories: Low VA, low KCO -> labelled Mixed (alveolar volume per-alveolus gas exchange reduced). Low VA, normal--high KCO -> labelled Volume loss (fewer alveoli, exchanging gas normally per unit volume). descriptive labels z-score pattern; clinical interpretation underlies pattern reader’s job.

title: Glossary
text: FEV1 — Forced Expiratory Volume 1 second. volume air expired first second forced expiratory maneuver, litres. FVC — Forced Vital Capacity. total volume air expired forced exhalation full inspiration, litres. FEV1/FVC — Ratio FEV1 FVC. Dimensionless; typical adult values 0.70 – 0.85. FEF25-75 — Forced Expiratory Flow 25% 75% FVC. Litres per second. FEF75 — Forced Expiratory Flow 75% expired FVC. FRC — Functional Residual Capacity. volume lungs end normal tidal expiration. TLC — Total Lung Capacity. maximum volume air lungs full inspiration. RV — Residual Volume. volume remaining lungs maximum exhalation. RV/TLC — ratio residual volume total lung capacity. ERV — Expiratory Reserve Volume. IC — Inspiratory Capacity. VC — Vital Capacity (slow VC, distinct FVC). TLCO / DLCO — Transfer factor / diffusing capacity carbon monoxide. “TLCO” SI-units name; “DLCO” traditional-units name; describe physiological measure. KCO — Carbon-monoxide transfer coefficient, equal TLCO/VA (DLCO/VA). Expressed SI traditional units accordingly. VA — Alveolar volume, used single-breath DLCO maneuver.

title: Glossary
text: M (median / predicted value) — age-, sex-, demographic- adjusted central value measure. GLI LMS framework, expected value healthy individual matching inputs. S (coefficient variation) — scale parameter LMS framework captures spread healthy values age/sex combination. L (skewness / Box-Cox transform) — shape parameter LMS framework adjusts non-normality underlying distribution. LMS method — statistical framework (Cole TJ. Stat Med. 1988;7(3):305-12) models reference distributions via three age-varying parameters L, M, S. Used GLI equations FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC, TLCO, lung volumes, others. LLN (lower limit normal) — 5th percentile reference distribution: value healthy individual falls 5% time. Equivalent z-score −1.645. ULN (upper limit normal) — 95th percentile, equivalent z-score +1.645. z-score — measure far observed value predicted median, expressed standard deviation units. LMS framework: z=(measured/M)L−1L⋅S.z = \frac{(measured / M)^L - 1}{L \cdot S}. Implemented <measure>_zscore outputs pft_spirometry(), pft_volumes(), pft_diffusion(). Percent predicted — (measured / M) × 100. traditional expression departure predicted; superseded z-scores Stanojevic 2022 standard still widely used clinical practice.

title: Glossary
text: Normal — measured values LLN. Obstructed — FEV1/FVC LLN TLC LLN (unknown). Restricted — TLC LLN FEV1/FVC LLN. Mixed — FEV1/FVC TLC LLNs. Non-specific pattern — Low FVC normal FEV1/FVC normal TLC. definition restrictive (TLC normal) obstructive (FEV1/FVC normal). label descriptive ; Stanojevic 2022 Table 5 enumerates clinical contexts. PRISm — Preserved Ratio Impaired Spirometry. Low FEV1 FEV1/FVC LLN. spirometry-screening label (TLC required). Dysanapsis — normal-variant pattern normal FEV1, high FVC, low FEV1/FVC. Listed Stanojevic 2022 Table 5 emitted separate label pft_classify() (folded “Obstructed” FEV1/FVC LLN).

title: Glossary
text: BDR (bronchodilator response) — Per Stanojevic 2022: change 10% predicted value FEV1 FVC pre- post-bronchodilator measurements. Implemented pft_bdr(). Replaces 2005 standard (≥12% ≥200 mL baseline). Severity grading — Per Stanojevic 2022, uniform three-level system applied z-score: Implemented pft_severity(). GOLD COPD severity — Per Global Initiative Chronic Obstructive Lung Disease, patients confirmed airflow obstruction: Implemented pft_gold(). CCS (conditional change score) — z-score-style index whether change two measurements computed (z2 − r * z1) / sqrt(1 − r^2) exceeds within-subject variability expected regression---mean alone. |CCS| > 1.96 Stanojevic 2022 two-sided 95% normal-limits threshold (Box 2). Implemented pft_change(). Spirometry quality grade (–F) — Per Graham et al. ATS/ERS 2019, grade based number acceptable maneuvers session difference two best values: child thresholds (column “Best-two diff (child ≤ 6)”) additionally floored 10% highest measured value per Graham 2019 Table 10’s footnote. Implemented pft_quality().

title: Glossary
text: pft_classify() emits ats_pattern_combination column four-character string. character (LLN) N (LLN), fixed order: FEV1 FVC FEV1/FVC TLC "NNAN" means normal FEV1, normal FVC, abnormally low FEV1/FVC, normal TLC. definition pure airway obstruction.

title: Glossary
text: GLI 2012 distinguishes five ancestral groups spirometry: Caucasian — European-ancestry populations. AfrAm — African American. NEAsia — North-East Asian (Han Chinese, Japanese, Korean). SEAsia — South-East Asian. /mixed — multi-ethnic composite category constructed GLI Task Force populations captured four . GLI Global 2022 race-neutral; race column ignored calling pft_spirometry(year = 2022).

title: Glossary
text: cohort unexpectedly -NA see warning unrecognised inputs, check following anything else: Sex must canonical "M" / "F". Common dataset values like "male", "Male", "MALE", "m", "f", "Female", "woman", "boy", "girl" auto-normalised warning. Anything else (e.g. "Unknown", "X", "NB") set NA. Prior behaviour value "M" silently treated female; make sure data isn’t relying . Race must one five GLI 2012 categories. Common variants like "caucasian" (lowercase), " Caucasian" (whitespace), "white", "black", "African American", "european" auto-normalised warning. Anything else ("Asian" ambiguous NEAsia/SEAsia mapped NEAsia; strings like "Hispanic", "Latino", "Native American" GLI 2012 framework) set NA. year = 2012 without race column errors rather silently producing -NA output. Either supply race column call pft_spirometry(data, year = 2022) race-neutral equations.

title: Input data format
text: Three categories column drive reference functions: Required – demographics must present, function errors: pft_spirometry(year = 2012) pft_interpret(year = 2012): sex, age, height, race. pft_spirometry(year = 2022) pft_interpret(year = 2022): sex, age, height (GLI Global 2022 equations race-neutral ignore race). pft_volumes() pft_diffusion(): sex, age, height. Optional measured – <measure>_measured columns whose presence unlocks z-score percent-predicted outputs measure. Missing silent; function simply emits fewer output columns. Recognised measures function: Spirometry: fev1, fvc, fev1fvc, fef2575, fef75. Volumes: frc, tlc, rv, rv_tlc, erv, ic, vc. Diffusion (traditional units): dlco, kco_tr, va. Diffusion (SI units): tlco, kco_si, va. Optional BDR – (pft_interpret() ) <measure>_pre <measure>_post columns fev1, fvc, fev1fvc. present, bronchodilator-response columns appended.

Either supply one (canonical levels: "Caucasian", "AfrAm", "NEAsia", "SEAsia", "/mixed") call pft_spirometry(d, year = 2022) race-neutral equations. Warning: pft input normalization: ... – one sex race values normalised (e.g. "male" → "M") set NA didn’t match known canonical value. warning consolidated one message per call lists offending values can locate affected rows. year = 2012 running outputs NA – check whether race column contains values outside five GLI 2012 categories. Unknown values set NA, propagates reference equations.

title: Interpretation reference: severity bands, patterns, and standards
text: pft_severity() translates z-score one four bands per Stanojevic 2022 standard. cut-points come straight paper’s interpretation table: vectorised call: 2005 Pellegrino bands grade percent predicted FEV1 rather z-score five tiers (mild, moderate, moderately-severe, severe, -severe). appropriate reproducing legacy reports matching clinic’s existing severity-grading convention; use pft_severity_2005(): standard = c("2022", "2005") argument flows pft_classify() pft_interpret() whole report can re-rendered either standard without changing input data.
code: data.frame(   band     = c("normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe"),   z_lower  = c(-1.645, -2.5, -4,    -Inf),   z_upper  = c( Inf,   -1.645, -2.5, -4) ) #>       band z_lower z_upper #> 1   normal  -1.645     Inf #> 2     mild  -2.500  -1.645 #> 3 moderate  -4.000  -2.500 #> 4   severe    -Inf  -4.000 pft_severity(c(0.2, -1.7, -3.0, -5.0)) #> [1] "normal"   "mild"     "moderate" "severe" pft_severity_2005(c(85, 65, 55, 40, 30)) #> [1] "mild"              "moderate"          "moderately severe" #> [4] "severe"            "very severe"

title: Interpretation reference: severity bands, patterns, and standards
text: pft_classify() assigns one five interpretive patterns per Stanojevic 2022 Figure 8 / Table 5: Normal – FEV1/FVC, FVC, FEV1 >= LLN. Obstructed – FEV1/FVC < LLN. Restricted – FEV1/FVC >= LLN, FVC < LLN, TLC < LLN. Mixed – FEV1/FVC < LLN TLC < LLN. Non-specific – FEV1/FVC >= LLN, FVC < LLN, TLC >= LLN. spirometry-version pattern (TLC unavailable) PRISm, surfaced pft_prism(). TLC missing, classifier falls back spirometry-branches Table 5 (Normal, Obstructed, Non-specific / PRISm); Restricted Mixed require TLC. Reading row row: FEV1/FVC < LLN, TLC normal -> Obstructed. FEV1/FVC < LLN TLC < LLN -> Mixed. FEV1/FVC normal, FVC < LLN, TLC < LLN -> Restricted. FEV1/FVC normal, FVC < LLN, TLC normal -> Non-specific. Everything >= LLN -> Normal.

title: Interpretation reference: severity bands, patterns, and standards
text: two standards differ three ways: 2022 standard recommended default pft_interpret() applies default. Use 2005 path reproducing historical report matching EMR template built older flowchart – run pft_interpret(data, standard = "2005") get predecessor severity BDR outputs alongside pft_classify(standard = "2005")’s pattern labels.

title: Interpretation reference: severity bands, patterns, and standards
text: pattern Obstructed moderate severity. GOLD staging (FEV1 % predicted) classifies GOLD 2:
code: copd <- data.frame(   sex = "M", age = 68, height = 175, race = "Caucasian",   fev1_measured    = 1.6,   fvc_measured     = 3.0,   fev1fvc_measured = 1.6 / 3.0,   tlc_measured     = 6.8 ) r <- pft_interpret(copd) r[, c("ats_classification", "fev1_severity_2022", "fev1_zscore_2022",        "fev1_pctpred_2022")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   ats_classification fev1_severity_2022 fev1_zscore_2022 fev1_pctpred_2022 #>   <chr>              <chr>                         <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 Obstructed         moderate                      -2.80              53.2 pft_gold(r$fev1_pctpred_2022, fev1fvc = r$fev1fvc_measured) #> [1] "GOLD 2"

what: Applying vector helpers inside a data-frame workflow
title: Interpretation reference: severity bands, patterns, and standards
text: package splits public surface two kinds function: Data-frame helpers – pft_classify(), pft_prism(), pft_volume_subpattern(), pft_diffusion_interpret() – consume several paired columns simultaneously accept column-name overrides via NSE (bare name, string, !!var). Vector helpers – pft_severity(), pft_severity_2005(), pft_gold(), pft_fev1q(), pft_dlco_hb_correct(), pft_quality(), pft_change(), pft_bdr(), pft_bdr_2005() – take one numeric vectors return vector small per-row tibble. designed compose inside dplyr::mutate(). cohort run combines reference values severity, GOLD staging, bronchodilator response: Grading every z-score column one pass dplyr::across(). Use matches("_zscore") rather ends_with("_zscore") year-suffixed spirometry columns (fev1_zscore_2022) also caught: split exists data-frame helpers need read paired columns (value LLN/ULN, three z-scores ) need know find data, vector helpers operate single named column compose naturally mutate() expressions.
code: library(dplyr)  out <- pft_spirometry(cohort) |>   mutate(     fev1_severity_2022 = pft_severity(fev1_zscore_2022),     fvc_severity_2022  = pft_severity(fvc_zscore_2022),     gold          = pft_gold(fev1_pctpred_2022, fev1fvc = fev1fvc_measured),     bdr_sig       = pft_bdr(fev1_pre, fev1_post, fev1_pred_2022)$is_significant   ) out |>   mutate(across(matches("_zscore"), pft_severity, .names = "{.col}_severity"))

title: Longitudinal PFT analysis: change and FEV1Q
text: pft_change() evaluates whether change two FEV1 z-scores larger expected within-subject variability regression mean. formula (Box 2 Stanojevic 2022): CCS=z2−r⋅z11−r2,r=0.642−0.04⋅timeyears+0.020⋅ageyears. \text{CCS} = \frac{z_2 - r \cdot z_1}{\sqrt{1 - r^2}}, \quad r = 0.642 - 0.04 \cdot \text{time}_\text{years} + 0.020 \cdot \text{age}_\text{years}. |CCS| > 1.96 two-sided 95 % significance threshold: drop spread four years significant – autocorrelation falls, z-score change readily explained noise: Scope. formula derived pediatric / young-adult cohort; standard notes “yet validated, extended adults” allows “reasonable tool facilitate interpretation”. adults standard recommends FEV1Q instead (Box 3); see section 2 .
code: # Box 2 worked example: 14-year-old male, FEV1 z dropped from -0.78 # to -1.60 over 3 months. pft_change(z1 = -0.78, z2 = -1.60, age_t1 = 14, time_years = 0.25) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>     ccs r_used is_significant #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <lgl>          #> 1 -2.17  0.912 TRUE pft_change(z1 = -0.78, z2 = -1.60, age_t1 = 14, time_years = 4) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>     ccs r_used is_significant #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <lgl>          #> 1 -1.55  0.762 FALSE

title: Longitudinal PFT analysis: change and FEV1Q
text: adults, 2022 standard recommends FEV1Q conditional change score (Box 3). FEV1Q ratio FEV1 sex-specific denominator (0.5 L males, 0.4 L females – 1st percentile adult lung-disease FEV1 distribution per Box 3). change score; expresses FEV1 absolute terms relative denominator, scale ’s comparable across patients time. function refuses age < 18 returning NA_real_ per paper’s “appropriate children adolescents” caveat. See Stanojevic 2022 Box 3 source standard’s interpretation resulting ratio.
code: # Box 3 worked example: 70-year-old female with FEV1 = 0.9 L. pft_fev1q(fev1 = 0.9, sex = "F", age = 70) #> [1] 2.25

title: Longitudinal PFT analysis: change and FEV1Q
text: pft_plot() produces single-patient lollipop figures. longitudinal trajectories, pipe long-form pft_long() (hand-built) table ggplot2 directly:
code: library(ggplot2) serial <- data.frame(   patient_id  = rep(1:2, each = 4),   visit_date  = rep(as.Date(c("2020-01-15","2021-03-10",                                "2022-05-20","2023-07-30")), 2),   fev1_zscore_2022 = c(-0.5, -0.8, -1.2, -1.6,                    0.2,  0.0, -0.3, -0.5) ) ggplot(serial, aes(visit_date, fev1_zscore_2022,                    colour = factor(patient_id), group = patient_id)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = -1.645, linetype = "dotted") +   geom_line() + geom_point() +   labs(x = "Visit date", y = "FEV1 z-score", colour = "Patient") +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting started with pft
text: simplest call: pass age, sex, height (race GLI 2012) get predicted values, lower limits normal (LLN), upper limits normal (ULN) every measure. default GLI 2022 (“GLI Global”), race-neutral equation set recommended ERS/ATS 2022 standard. use predecessor GLI 2012 multi-ethnic equations, pass year = 2012 include race column. pattern works lung volumes diffusion:
code: patient <- data.frame(   sex = "M", age = 45, height = 178 )  ref <- pft_spirometry(patient) ref[, c("fev1_pred_2022", "fev1_lln_2022", "fev1_uln_2022",         "fvc_pred_2022",  "fvc_lln_2022",  "fvc_uln_2022")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   fev1_pred_2022 fev1_lln_2022 fev1_uln_2022 fvc_pred_2022 fvc_lln_2022 #>            <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1           3.87          2.94          4.75          4.81         3.68 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: fvc_uln_2022 <dbl> pft_volumes(patient)[, c("frc_pred", "tlc_pred", "rv_pred", "vc_pred")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   frc_pred tlc_pred rv_pred vc_pred #>      <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1     3.39     7.21    1.72    5.50 pft_diffusion(patient)[, c("dlco_pred", "kco_tr_pred", "va_pred")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   dlco_pred kco_tr_pred va_pred #>       <dbl>       <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      30.3        4.58    6.67

what: 2. Z-scores and percent predicted from measured values
title: Getting started with pft
text: Add <measure>_measured columns z-scores percent-predicted appear automatically next reference values. z-score uses standard LMS formula ((measured / M)^L - 1) / (L * S). Percent predicted (measured / M) * 100.
code: patient_with_measurements <- data.frame(   sex = "M", age = 45, height = 178, race = "Caucasian",   fev1_measured = 2.5,   fvc_measured  = 3.8 )  out <- pft_spirometry(patient_with_measurements) out[, c("fev1_pred_2022", "fev1_zscore_2022", "fev1_pctpred_2022",         "fvc_pred_2022",  "fvc_zscore_2022",  "fvc_pctpred_2022")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   fev1_pred_2022 fev1_zscore_2022 fev1_pctpred_2022 fvc_pred_2022 #>            <dbl>            <dbl>             <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1           3.87            -2.39              64.6          4.81 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: fvc_zscore_2022 <dbl>, fvc_pctpred_2022 <dbl>

title: Getting started with pft
text: pft_severity() maps z-score one four categories per Stanojevic 2022 cut points: can grade z-score column directly:
code: pft_severity(c(0, -1.7, -3, -5)) #> [1] "normal"   "mild"     "moderate" "severe" out$fev1_severity_2022 <- pft_severity(out$fev1_zscore_2022) out$fvc_severity_2022  <- pft_severity(out$fvc_zscore_2022) out[, c("fev1_zscore_2022", "fev1_severity_2022", "fvc_zscore_2022", "fvc_severity_2022")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   fev1_zscore_2022 fev1_severity_2022 fvc_zscore_2022 fvc_severity_2022 #>              <dbl> <chr>                        <dbl> <chr>             #> 1            -2.39 mild                         -1.47 normal

title: Getting started with pft
text: Given measured spirometry plus TLC LLNs, pft_classify() labels pattern per Stanojevic 2022 Figure 8: 4-character ats_pattern_combination records inputs drove label (= abnormal / LLN, N = LLN), order FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC, TLC. ANAN means FEV1 FEV1/FVC low; FVC TLC normal.
code: classification_input <- data.frame(   fev1 = 2.5,  fev1_lln_2022 = 3.0,   fvc  = 3.8,  fvc_lln_2022  = 3.5,   fev1fvc = 0.66, fev1fvc_lln_2022 = 0.70,   tlc  = 6.0,  tlc_lln = 5.0 ) pft_classify(classification_input)[   , c("ats_classification", "ats_pattern_combination") ] #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   ats_classification ats_pattern_combination #>   <chr>              <chr>                   #> 1 Obstructed         ANAN

title: Getting started with pft
text: Stanojevic 2022 BDR criterion >10% change relative predicted FEV1 FVC (replacing 2005 12% / 200 mL rule):
code: pft_bdr(pre = 2.5, post = 3.0, predicted = 4.0) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   pct_pred_change is_significant #>             <dbl> <lgl>          #> 1            12.5 TRUE

title: Getting started with pft
text: longitudinal monitoring, conditional change score (CCS) adjusts regression mean using within-subject z-score autocorrelation r. |CCS| > 1.96 (Stanojevic 2022 two-sided 95% threshold) indicates change outside normal-limits range.
code: # z dropped from -0.5 to -2.5 over 1 year; r ≈ 0.7 for adult FEV1 pft_change(z1 = -0.5, z2 = -2.5, r = 0.7) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>     ccs r_used is_significant #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <lgl>          #> 1 -3.01    0.7 TRUE

title: Getting started with pft
text: pft_interpret() auto-detects every available input produces full Stanojevic 2022-compliant interpretation one call:
code: patient <- data.frame(   sex = "M", age = 45, height = 178, race = "Caucasian",   fev1_measured     = 2.5,   fvc_measured      = 3.8,   fev1fvc_measured  = 2.5 / 3.8,   tlc_measured      = 6.0,   fev1_pre          = 2.5,   fev1_post         = 2.9 ) result <- pft_interpret(patient)  # A high-level subset of the ~60 columns generated: result[, c("fev1_pred_2022", "fev1_zscore_2022", "fev1_severity_2022",            "fvc_zscore_2022", "fvc_severity_2022",            "ats_classification", "prism",            "fev1_bdr_pct", "fev1_bdr_significant")] #> <pft_result> #>  Measure     Pred  Measured Z     Severity #>  FEV1 (2022)  3.87 -        -2.39 mild     #>  #> Pattern: Obstructed #> PRISm: FALSE #> BDR FEV1: TRUE ( 10.3% of predicted) #>  #> Use `as_tibble(x)` or `as.data.frame(x)` for the full output (9 columns).

title: Getting started with pft
text: Everything composes naturally pipeline. Apply pft_interpret() multi-row data frame output data frame ~60 interpretation columns appended:
code: cohort <- data.frame(   sex    = c("M", "F", "M"),   age    = c(45, 60, 30),   height = c(178, 165, 175),   race   = c("Caucasian", "AfrAm", "Caucasian"),   fev1_measured = c(2.5, 1.8, 4.0),   fvc_measured  = c(3.8, 2.4, 5.2),   fev1fvc_measured = c(2.5/3.8, 1.8/2.4, 4.0/5.2),   tlc_measured  = c(6.0, 4.5, 6.8) )  interpreted <- pft_interpret(cohort) interpreted[, c("sex", "age",                 "fev1_zscore_2022", "fev1_severity_2022",                 "ats_classification", "prism")] #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   sex     age fev1_zscore_2022 fev1_severity_2022 ats_classification prism #>   <chr> <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>              <chr>              <lgl> #> 1 M        45           -2.39  mild               Obstructed         FALSE #> 2 F        60           -1.58  normal             Normal             FALSE #> 3 M        30           -0.122 normal             Normal             FALSE

title: Getting started with pft
text: pft_long() pivots wide pft_result one row per (patient, measure), natural shape dplyr / ggplot2 workflows. S3 method tidy.pft_result() dispatches broom installed, broom::tidy(interpreted) identical pft_long(interpreted).

title: pft — ATS/ERS 2022-compliant pulmonary function test interpretation
text: pft implements Stanojevic 2022 ERS/ATS interpretive standard pulmonary function tests. Reference values come GLI family equations (GLI 2012 GLI Global 2022 spirometry, GLI 2021 static lung volumes, GLI 2017 TLCO 2020 author correction). top , package computes z-scores percent predicted, ATS pattern classification, severity grading, bronchodilator response, PRISm, conditional change scores longitudinal slopes, GLI 2012 vs GLI Global 2022 reclassification, Hughes & Pride diffusion category, lung age, ggplot2-based single-patient cohort figures.

title: pft — ATS/ERS 2022-compliant pulmonary function test interpretation
text: reference function emits <measure>_pred, <measure>_lln, <measure>_uln. <measure>_measured column also present, <measure>_zscore <measure>_pctpred appended automatically. Spirometry outputs additionally carry four-digit GLI-year suffix (e.g. fev1_pred_2012, fev1_pred_2022) single result frame can hold GLI 2012 GLI Global 2022 columns side--side, suffix isn’t ambiguous default year changes future release. title: pft: Pulmonary Function Test Interpretation per ERS/ATS 2022 — pft-package
text: support unambiguous clinical research use, following conventions hold across every exported function: Sex canonically coded character string "M" "F". Common variants ("male", "Female", "m", "woman", etc.) auto-normalised warning. Truly unrecognised values ("X", "Unknown", etc.) treated missing. Age years (decimal allowed). Height centimetres. Volumes (FEV1, FVC, FRC, TLC, RV, ERV, IC, VC) litres. DLCO / TLCO reported units function emits (controlled SI.units pft_diffusion()). z-scores use LMS-based formula z = ((measured / M)^L - 1) / (L * S) SIGNED values 0 predicted median. Lower limit normal (LLN) 5th percentile reference distribution; upper limit normal (ULN) 95th percentile. correspond z-scores -1.645 +1.645 respectively. NA handling: rows missing sex, age, height get -NA outputs. applies race GLI 2012 path. NA inputs propagate naturally arithmetic. 4-character pattern combination emitted pft_classify() uses positions FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC, TLC, order, "" denoting LLN "N" denoting . "NNAN" means FEV1/FVC LLN.

title: pft: Pulmonary Function Test Interpretation per ERS/ATS 2022 — pft-package
text: pft currently cover: Reference equations outside GLI family (NHANES III, JRS, ECSC, Knudson, Crapo, Hankinson, etc.). Use rspiro package NHANES III JRS. Ethnic groups outside five recognised GLI 2012 (Caucasian, African American, North-East Asian, South-East Asian, /mixed). GLI Global 2022 equations race-neutral apply across ancestral groups. Pre-school spirometry (ages < 3). Bronchial challenge testing (PC20 methacholine, mannitol). Cardiopulmonary exercise testing. Six-minute walk test interpretation. Sleep-study integration. Pediatric-specific bronchodilator-response thresholds (2022 adult criterion applied uniformly). Sub-patterns within Stanojevic 2022 algorithm coarse 5-category classifier enumerate (Dysanapsis, etc.). package's pft_classify() folds Dysanapsis "Obstructed" FEV1/FVC LLN. Altitude corrections DLCO. (Haemoglobin correction supported – see pft_dlco_hb_correct().) Sex categories outside "M" "F". Outputs intended research education. Clinical decisions made qualified clinicians using validated tools; pft FDA-cleared, medical device, validated diagnostic decision-making.

what: Conditional change score for serial PFT measurements — pft_change
title: Conditional change score for serial PFT measurements — pft_change
text: pft_change() computes conditional change score (CCS) defined Box 2 Stanojevic et al. ERS/ATS 2022 interpretation standard. CCS evaluates whether change two FEV1 z-scores larger expected within-subject variability regression mean alone. Formula (paper Box 2 p. 12): $$CCS = (z_2 - r \cdot z_1) / \sqrt{1 - r^2}$$ autocorrelation r function time interval measurements patient's age first time point: $$r = 0.642 - 0.04 \cdot time(years) + 0.020 \cdot age(years)$$ Changes within +/- 1.96 change scores considered within normal limits per paper. formula derived children/young-people cohort (Stanojevic 2022 references underlying study notes approach "yet validated, extended adults" permits use "reasonable tool facilitate interpretation"). adults 2022 standard alternatively recommends FEV1Q (Box 3); see pft_fev1q().

title: Conditional change score for serial PFT measurements — pft_change
code: pft_change(z1, z2, age_t1 = NULL, time_years = NULL, r = NULL)

title: Conditional change score for serial PFT measurements — pft_change
text: z1, z2 Numeric vectors FEV1 z-scores time 1 time 2. age_t1 Numeric. Patient age (years) first measurement. time_years Numeric. Elapsed time measurements years (e.g. 0.25 3 months, 4 4 years). r Optional. Numeric (-1, 1). supplied, used directly place paper's age/time formula – useful callers population-specific autocorrelation estimate. NULL (default), r computed age_t1 time_years via Box 2 formula.

title: Conditional change score for serial PFT measurements — pft_change

title: Conditional change score for serial PFT measurements — pft_change

title: Classify ATS spirometry patterns from spirometry and lung-volume measurements — pft_classify
text: pft_classify() assigns ATS patterns using spirometry lung volume data. default applies Stanojevic et al. ERS/ATS 2022 algorithm (Figure 8); pass standard = "2005" apply predecessor Pellegrino et al. ERJ 2005 algorithm. Typically called via pft_interpret() part one-call workflow; exported callers want apply classifier pre-computed columns directly.

title: Classify ATS spirometry patterns from spirometry and lung-volume measurements — pft_classify
code: pft_classify(   data,   standard = c("2022", "2005"),   year = 2022,   fev1 = fev1,   fev1_lln = NULL,   fvc = fvc,   fvc_lln = NULL,   fev1fvc = fev1fvc,   fev1fvc_lln = NULL,   tlc = tlc,   tlc_lln = tlc_lln )

title: Classify ATS spirometry patterns from spirometry and lung-volume measurements — pft_classify
text: data data frame containing six spirometry input columns (fev1, fev1_lln, fvc, fvc_lln, fev1fvc, fev1fvc_lln) optionally tlc tlc_lln. TLC columns optional – either absent data, classifier routes via spirometry-fallback (see "Missing TLC" ). standard interpretive standard's classifier apply. "2022" (default) follows Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2022 Figure 8 recognises five labels: Normal, Non-specific, Obstructed, Restricted, Mixed. "2005" follows Pellegrino et al. ERJ 2005 Figure 2 recognises four labels (Normal, Obstructed, Restricted, Mixed). 2005 algorithm consults TLC FVC LLN; FVC normal routes directly Normal Obstructed regardless TLC. dominant source 2005 -> 2022 reclassification: rows low TLC normal FVC (NNNA, ANNA) become Restricted 2022 stay Normal 2005; rows low FEV1/FVC low TLC normal FVC (NNAA, ANAA) become Mixed 2022 stay Obstructed 2005; isolated-low-FVC cells (NANN, AANN) become Non-specific 2022 Normal 2005. year GLI year suffix use looking spirometry LLN columns (fev1_lln, fvc_lln, fev1fvc_lln). Defaults 2022 (GLI Global, race-neutral). Set match year argument used upstream pft_spirometry() / pft_interpret() call. TLC columns (volumes reference) unsuffixed affected year. fev1, fev1_lln, fvc, fvc_lln, fev1fvc, fev1fvc_lln, tlc, tlc_lln Column references eight inputs. Defaults canonical names (fev1, fev1_lln, ...); override bare name, string, !!var (see "Column-name overrides" ).

title: Classify ATS spirometry patterns from spirometry and lung-volume measurements — pft_classify
text: original data frame two appended columns: ats_classification: pattern label. Values depend selected standard; see . ats_pattern_combination: 4-character string fixed column order FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC, TLC, "" denoting value LLN, "N" denoting , "?" denoting value (LLN) missing. "NNAN" means FEV1/FVC LLN (pure airway obstruction); "AANA" means FEV1, FVC, TLC low FEV1/FVC preserved (restriction); "NNA?" means FEV1/FVC LLN TLC unknown. pattern-combination string independent standard selected.

title: Classify ATS spirometry patterns from spirometry and lung-volume measurements — pft_classify
text: column-reference argument accepts three forms: bare column name – fev1 = my_fev1 string – fev1 = "my_fev1" injected value – fev1 = !!my_var my_var <- "my_fev1" Defaults canonical pft column names, callers whose data already follows convention pass extra arguments. two TLC references (tlc, tlc_lln) optional: either resolves column present data, spirometry-fallback triggers without raising error.

title: Classify ATS spirometry patterns from spirometry and lung-volume measurements — pft_classify
text: three spirometry inputs (fev1, fvc, fev1fvc) LLNs present TLC missing, pft_classify() falls back spirometry-branch instead returning NA. standards, "Obstructed" row still recognisable FEV1/FVC < LLN alone (Mixed require TLC distinguish Mixed obstructive defect, row labelled "Obstructed"). 2005 standard, rows FVC \(\ge\) LLN classify deterministically 2005 flowchart consult TLC branch ("Normal" emitted normal spirometry). Cells TLC disambiguating input (Normal vs Restricted, Non-specific vs Restricted 2022; Normal vs Restricted, Obstructed vs Mixed 2005) remain NA. Rows spirometry input missing always return NA. See pft_prism() spirometry-PRISm screen reported separate logical column.

code: pft_diffusion(data, SI.units = FALSE, sex = sex, age = age, height = height)

title: Compute carbon monoxide diffusion capacity or transfer factor reference values for given demographics — pft_diffusion
text: data data frame containing columns sex ("M","F"), age (years, range 5-90 per GLI 2017 spline tables) height (centimeters). data also contains <measure>_measured column active measures (tlco, kco_si, va SI units; dlco, kco_tr, va traditional), measured value used compute z-score percent-predicted (see Value). SI.units boolean. Returns reference values SI units TRUE .  Traditional units FALSE. sex, age, height Column references. default pft_diffusion() reads sex, age, height. Override via bare name (sex = Sex), string (sex = "Sex"), rlang injection (sex = !!my_var). user's original column names preserved output.

title: Compute carbon monoxide diffusion capacity or transfer factor reference values for given demographics — pft_diffusion
text: original data frame extra columns appended measure: <measure>_pred: predicted (median) value. <measure>_lln:  lower limit normal (5th percentile). <measure>_uln:  upper limit normal (95th percentile). <measure>_measured column supplied data, two additional columns emitted: <measure>_zscore:  LMS z-score ((measured/M)^L - 1) / (L*S). <measure>_pctpred: percent predicted (measured / pred) * 100.

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
text: Takes per-patient dlco_zscore, va_zscore, kco_zscore columns (outputs pft_diffusion() _measured columns supplied) assigns clinical interpretive category per Hughes & Pride 2012 framework adopted ERS/ATS Stanojevic 2017 task force.

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
code: pft_diffusion_interpret(   data,   SI.units = FALSE,   dlco = NULL,   va = NULL,   kco = NULL )

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
text: data data frame containing three z-score input columns. SI.units Logical, default FALSE. Selects default column names dlco kco. Traditional units (FALSE): dlco_zscore kco_tr_zscore. SI units (TRUE): tlco_zscore kco_si_zscore. va defaults va_zscore unit systems. dlco, va, kco Column references three z-score inputs. dlco kco default NULL, means: pick canonical column name based SI.units. Pass bare name, string, !!var override (see "Column-name overrides").

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
text: original data frame single appended column: diffusion_category. Possible values: "Normal" three z-scores LLN. "Parenchymal" Low DLCO, low KCO, normal VA. "Volume loss" Low DLCO, low VA, normal elevated KCO. "Mixed" Low DLCO, low VA, low KCO. "Vascular (suggested)" Low DLCO, normal VA, low elevated KCO. "Elevated KCO" Normal DLCO elevated KCO (z > +1.645). "" Combination matching patterns (e.g., low VA isolation). NA Required z-score columns missing. category labels describe z-score pattern ; differential diagnosis left clinician (see Hughes & Pride 2012 source paper clinical interpretation).

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
text: classifier consumes z-scores unit-agnostic, default input column names differ unit systems. Set SI.units = TRUE pick SI-units column set (tlco_zscore, va_zscore, kco_si_zscore); otherwise traditional-units column set (dlco_zscore, va_zscore, kco_tr_zscore) used. Override individual column names via dlco / va / kco arguments. Typically called via pft_interpret() part one-call workflow; exported callers want apply classifier pre-computed z-score columns directly.

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
text: column-reference argument accepts three forms: bare column name – dlco = my_dlco string – dlco = "my_dlco" injected value – dlco = !!my_var my_var <- "my_dlco" dlco kco default NULL, selects canonical name based SI.units (traditional SI). Passing explicit reference overrides selection.

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
text: Hughes JM, Pride NB. Examination carbon monoxide diffusing capacity (DL(CO)) relation KCO VA components. J Respir Crit Care Med. 2012;186(2):132-139. doi:10.1164/rccm.201112-2160CI . Stanojevic S, Graham BL, Cooper BG, et al. ERS/ATS technical standard: Global Lung Function Initiative reference values carbon monoxide transfer factor Caucasians. Eur Respir J. 2017;50:1700010. doi:10.1183/13993003.00010-2017 . (Provides z-score reference standard whose LLN z = -1.645 used . clinical interpretation framework Hughes & Pride 2012, adopted 2017 task force.)

title: Classify a diffusion result into a clinical pattern category — pft_diffusion_interpret
code: # Three patients: normal, parenchymal (low DLCO/KCO, normal VA), # and volume loss (low DLCO/VA, normal KCO). d <- data.frame(   dlco_zscore  = c(-0.5, -2.0, -2.0),   va_zscore    = c(-0.5, -0.5, -2.0),   kco_tr_zscore = c(-0.5, -2.0, -0.5) ) pft_diffusion_interpret(d) #>   dlco_zscore va_zscore kco_tr_zscore diffusion_category #> 1        -0.5      -0.5          -0.5             Normal #> 2        -2.0      -0.5          -2.0        Parenchymal #> 3        -2.0      -2.0          -0.5        Volume loss  # SI units (TLCO / KCO_SI). Pass SI.units = TRUE. d_si <- data.frame(   tlco_zscore   = c(-0.5, -2.0),   va_zscore     = c(-0.5, -0.5),   kco_si_zscore = c(-0.5, -2.0) ) pft_diffusion_interpret(d_si, SI.units = TRUE) #>   tlco_zscore va_zscore kco_si_zscore diffusion_category #> 1        -0.5      -0.5          -0.5             Normal #> 2        -2.0      -0.5          -2.0        Parenchymal

what: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
title: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
text: Applies Cotes 1972 hemoglobin correction measured carbon monoxide transfer factor (DLCO TLCO), returning value measured patient's hemoglobin equaled age- sex-specific reference. correction intended clinical interpretation: applying , corrected DLCO can passed pft_diffusion() (whose reference equations assume reference Hb).

title: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
code: pft_dlco_hb_correct(dlco, hemoglobin, sex, age = NA_real_)

title: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
text: dlco Numeric vector measured DLCO TLCO values, unit system (correction multiplicative unit-agnostic). hemoglobin Numeric vector measured hemoglobin g/L (routine clinical adult range 120-160 g/L). Pass g/L directly; function detect convert g/dL inputs. sex Character vector ("M"/"F"). Soft-corrected via internal normalize_sex_vec() helper "Male", "female", etc. work. age Optional numeric vector. supplied, males aged < 15 yr use female / child reference (134 g/L) per Stanojevic 2017 p. 11. omitted (default NA_real_), males default adult reference (146).

title: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
text: Numeric vector Hb-corrected DLCO / TLCO values, units dlco. NA propagates input.

title: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
text: Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2017 explicitly recommends (Table 4 p. 9) published GLI TLCO reference values uncorrected Hb, Hb levels considered separately interpretation. function provides interpretive step. Formula (Cotes 1972; reformulated Stanojevic 2017 p. 9, constants 1.7 0.7 reflecting membrane / capillary diffusing capacity ratio 0.7 mL min⁻¹ mmHg⁻¹ mL-blood⁻¹): $$TLCO_{Hb} = TLCO_{measured} \times \frac{1.7 \cdot Hb_{ref}}{Hb + 0.7 \cdot Hb_{ref}}$$ Reference Hb levels (Stanojevic 2017 p. 11): Males aged >= 15 yr: 146 g/L Females (age) children < 15 yr: 134 g/L hemoglobin == Hb_ref correction factor exactly 1 (adjustment). Anaemic patients (hemoglobin < Hb_ref) receive UPWARD correction reflecting less Hb means less CO uptake depressed measured value.

title: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
text: Stanojevic S, Graham BL, Cooper BG, et al. Official ERS technical standards: Global Lung Function Initiative reference values carbon monoxide transfer factor Caucasians. Eur Respir J. 2017;50(3):1700010. doi:10.1183/13993003.00010-2017 . Hb correction discussed p. 9 Cotes formula context p. 11. Cotes JE, Dabbs JM, Elwood PC, et al. Iron-deficiency anaemia: effect transfer factor lung (diffusing capacity) ventilation cardiac frequency sub-maximal exercise. Clin Sci. 1972;42:325-335.

title: Adjust a measured DLCO / TLCO for the patient's hemoglobin — pft_dlco_hb_correct
code: # An anaemic adult male (Hb = 100 g/L vs reference 146 g/L) with # measured DLCO of 20 mL/min/mmHg has a Hb-adjusted DLCO of # 20 x (1.7 x 146) / (100 + 0.7 x 146) ~= 24.55. pft_dlco_hb_correct(dlco = 20, hemoglobin = 100,                     sex = "M", age = 40) #> [1] 24.54995  # No-op when Hb equals reference. pft_dlco_hb_correct(20, hemoglobin = 146, sex = "M") #> [1] 20  # Children use the female / child reference (134 g/L) regardless # of sex. pft_dlco_hb_correct(20, hemoglobin = 134, sex = "M", age = 10) #> [1] 20

what: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
title: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
text: Computes FEV1Q survival index proposed Miller & Pedersen (ERJ 2010) discussed adult alternative conditional change score Box 3 (p. 13) Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2022 interpretation standard. FEV1Q expresses FEV1 relation "bottom line" required survival, rather far individual's result predicted value; race-neutral evidence base consolidated Balasubramanian et al. (ERJ 2024).

title: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
code: pft_fev1q(fev1, sex, age = NA_real_)

title: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
text: fev1 Numeric vector FEV1 measurements litres. sex Character vector patient sex. Accepts soft- correctable variants pft_spirometry() ("Male", "female", etc.); unrecognized values yield NA. age Optional numeric vector. supplied, rows age < 18 return NA_real_ per paper's "appropriate children adolescents" caveat. Default NA_real_ skips guard.

title: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
text: Numeric vector FEV1Q ratios, length fev1. NA propagates input.

title: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
text: Formula (Box 3 verbatim): $$FEV1Q = FEV1 / Q_{sex}$$ \(Q_{male} = 0.5 L\) \(Q_{female} = 0.4 L\) sex-specific 1st percentiles FEV1 distribution adult lung-disease populations. index approximates number turnovers remaining lower survivable limit FEV1; values closer 1 indicate greater risk death. 2022 standard cautions (running text p. 13, immediately preceding Box 3): "FEV1Q appropriate children adolescents." age supplied, rows age < 18 return NA_real_. age omitted, age guard skipped caller responsible restricting input adults. longitudinal interpretation adults 2022 standard suggests FEV1Q alternative conditional change score (see pft_change()): normal circumstances 1 unit FEV1Q lost approximately every 18 years (every ~10 years smokers elderly).

title: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
text: Miller MR, Pedersen . New concepts expressing forced expiratory volume 1 s arising survival analysis. Eur Respir J. 2010;35(4):873-882. doi:10.1183/09031936.00025809 . Original proposal FEV1Q index sex-specific 1st-percentile denominators (0.5 L male, 0.4 L female). Balasubramanian , Wise RA, Stanojevic S, Miller MR, McCormack MC. FEV1Q: race-neutral approach assessing lung function. Eur Respir J. 2024;63(4):2301622. doi:10.1183/13993003.01622-2023 . Race-neutral validation FEV1Q index. Stanojevic S, Kaminsky DA, Miller MR, et al. ERS/ATS technical standard interpretive strategies routine lung function tests. Eur Respir J. 2022;60(1):2101499. doi:10.1183/13993003.01499-2021 . Discusses FEV1Q Box 3 (p. 13) adult alternative conditional change score.

title: FEV1Q: ratio of FEV1 to a sex-specific survivable lower limit — pft_fev1q
code: # Stanojevic 2022 Box 3 worked example: a 70-year-old woman with # FEV1 of 0.9 L has FEV1Q of 0.9 / 0.4 = 2.25. pft_fev1q(0.9, "F", age = 70) #> [1] 2.25  # Vectorised across sex. pft_fev1q(c(1.0, 1.0), c("M", "F")) #> [1] 2.0 2.5  # Adolescents return NA when age is supplied. pft_fev1q(1.0, "F", age = 10) #> [1] NA

what: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
title: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
text: Returns GOLD spirometric severity grade (1-4) one patients given FEV1 expressed percent predicted, optionally enforcing GOLD-mandated prerequisite confirmed airflow obstruction (FEV1/FVC < 0.7).

title: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
code: pft_gold(fev1_pctpred, fev1fvc = NA_real_)

title: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
text: fev1_pctpred Numeric vector FEV1 % predicted values (e.g. fev1_pctpred column pft_spirometry() measured values supplied). fev1fvc Optional numeric vector post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC ratios (e.g. fev1fvc_measured column). supplied, rows fev1fvc >= 0.7 returned NA_character_ – per GOLD 2026 Figure 2.10, grading applies "patients COPD (FEV1/FVC < 0.7)". omitted (default NA_real_) -NA, prerequisite check performed grade returned every non-NA fev1_pctpred.

title: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
text: Character vector values "GOLD 1", "GOLD 2", "GOLD 3", "GOLD 4", NA. NA returned rows missing fev1_pctpred (fev1fvc supplied) rows fail airflow-obstruction prerequisite.

title: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
text: GOLD severity grades airflow obstruction (Figure 2.10 GOLD 2026 report, content page 38): GOLD specifies prerequisite "patients COPD (FEV1/FVC < 0.7)" explicitly Figure 2.10's grade table; surrounding text (content p. 37) repeats requirement. Supplying fev1fvc enforces GOLD fixed-cutoff prerequisite. Callers wanting LLN-based prerequisite instead use pft_classify() identify obstructed patients mask pft_gold() output hand.

title: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
text: Global Initiative Chronic Obstructive Lung Disease (GOLD). Global Strategy Diagnosis, Management Prevention Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease, 2026 Report. Figure 2.10. https://goldcopd.org.

title: Grade COPD severity by GOLD criteria — pft_gold
code: # Without prerequisite check (backward-compatible): one grade per # non-NA input. pft_gold(c(85, 65, 40, 25)) #> [1] "GOLD 1" "GOLD 2" "GOLD 3" "GOLD 4" # -> "GOLD 1" "GOLD 2" "GOLD 3" "GOLD 4"  # With prerequisite check: the third patient has FEV1/FVC = 0.75 # (no airflow obstruction) and is returned NA. pft_gold(c(85, 65, 40, 25), fev1fvc = c(0.65, 0.60, 0.75, 0.55)) #> [1] "GOLD 1" "GOLD 2" NA       "GOLD 4" # -> "GOLD 1" "GOLD 2" NA "GOLD 4"

what: Comprehensive ERS/ATS 2022 PFT interpretation in one call — pft_interpret
title: Comprehensive ERS/ATS 2022 PFT interpretation in one call — pft_interpret
text: pft_interpret() single-call workflow combines every interpretation primitive package complete clinical report per Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2022 standard. auto-detects computations possible input columns skips anything : sex / age / height (race, year = 2012) present, computes spirometry reference values via pft_spirometry(). sex / age / height present, computes lung-volume reference values via pft_volumes(). sex / age / height present, computes diffusion reference values via pft_diffusion(). measure whose _measured column present, z-score percent-predicted appended (see individual reference functions details). measure z-score, <measure>_severity column appended via pft_severity(). fev1_measured, fvc_measured, fev1fvc_measured, tlc_measured columns present, ATS pattern classifier (pft_classify()) labels row. tlc_measured, rv_tlc_measured, fev1fvc_measured present (LLNs / ULNs computable), lung- volume sub-pattern classifier (pft_volume_subpattern()) adds volume_subpattern column. frc_tlc_measured / frc_tlc_uln also present, volume ratios consulted per Stanojevic 2022 Figure 10. fev1_measured, fev1fvc_measured, LLNs resolvable, pft_prism() adds prism flag (independent TLC). <measure>_pre <measure>_post columns present spirometry measure, pft_bdr() adds <measure>_bdr_pct <measure>_bdr_significant columns. recommended entry point clinical-style reporting; individual reference interpretation functions exported callers need finer-grained control.

title: Comprehensive ERS/ATS 2022 PFT interpretation in one call — pft_interpret
code: pft_interpret(   data,   year = 2022,   SI.units = FALSE,   standard = c("2022", "2005"),   sex = sex,   age = age,   height = height,   race = race )

title: Comprehensive ERS/ATS 2022 PFT interpretation in one call — pft_interpret
text: data data frame containing whatever inputs available. See Details column-name conventions. year GLI spirometry equation year. Defaults 2022 (GLI Global, race-neutral). See pft_spirometry(). SI.units Whether report diffusion SI units. See pft_diffusion(). standard Interpretive standard whose downstream rules apply: "2022" (default) uses Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2022 pattern classification, severity grading, BDR; "2005" uses Pellegrino et al. ERJ 2005 predecessor. selected standard affect GLI reference equations (controlled year) – downstream interpretive logic. Useful reclassification analyses comparing two standards cohort. sex, age, height, race Column references. default pft_interpret() reads sex, age, height, (year = 2012) race. Override via bare name (sex = Sex), string (sex = "Sex"), rlang injection (sex = !!my_var). _measured, _pre, _post columns still auto-detected name overridable.

title: Comprehensive ERS/ATS 2022 PFT interpretation in one call — pft_interpret
text: original data frame every applicable reference value, z-score, percent predicted, severity grade, pattern label, PRISm flag, BDR result appended.

title: Comprehensive ERS/ATS 2022 PFT interpretation in one call — pft_interpret
text: trigger z-scores percent-predicted measure, include corresponding <measure>_measured column data (e.g. fev1_measured, frc_measured, dlco_measured). trigger BDR, include <measure>_pre <measure>_post columns FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC. outputs trace specific equation, table, figure Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2022 underlying GLI reference papers; see @references blocks individual functions.

what: Clinical visualisation for a single PFT result — pft_plot
title: Clinical visualisation for a single PFT result — pft_plot
text: pft_plot() draws single-patient z-score figure: one row per measure, points patient's z-score, shaded reference bands four Stanojevic 2022 severity grades returned pft_severity(): normal (z >= -1.645), mild (-2.5 <= z < -1.645), moderate (-4 <= z < -2.5), severe (z < -4). normal band extends symmetrically zero upper limit normal; values ULN shown 2022 severity grade. Requires ggplot2 package (Suggested dependency).

what: Grade spirometry quality per ATS/ERS 2019 — pft_quality
title: Grade spirometry quality per ATS/ERS 2019 — pft_quality
text: Assigns one grades -F set acceptable spirometry maneuvers single measure (FEV1 FVC) per Graham et al. ATS/ERS 2019 technical standard, Table 10. Grades depend number acceptable maneuvers difference best two values.

title: Grade spirometry quality per ATS/ERS 2019 — pft_quality
code: pft_quality(values, age = NA_real_)

title: Grade spirometry quality per ATS/ERS 2019 — pft_quality
text: values Numeric vector measurements (litres) acceptable maneuver ONE patient ONE measure. Length 0 allowed yields grade "F". age Patient age, years. repeatability thresholds tighten children aged 6 younger; threshold greater absolute child value (0.100 / 0.150 / 0.200 L / C / D) 10% highest measured value, per Table 10's footnote. Defaults NA_real_, uses adult thresholds.

title: Grade spirometry quality per ATS/ERS 2019 — pft_quality
text: length-1 character value "", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F".

title: Grade spirometry quality per ATS/ERS 2019 — pft_quality
text: Grade definitions (Table 10, paper p. e83). Adult thresholds parentheses; child (age <= 6) thresholds max(absolute, 0.10 · max(values)): : >= 3 acceptable maneuvers; best two within 0.150 L (0.100 L child). B:    2 acceptable maneuvers; best two within 0.150 L (0.100 L child). C: >= 2 acceptable maneuvers; best two within 0.200 L (0.150 L child). D: >= 2 acceptable maneuvers; best two within 0.250 L (0.200 L child). E: >= 2 acceptable maneuvers best-two diff exceeding D threshold, exactly 1 acceptable maneuver. F: 0 acceptable maneuvers. Grade U ("0 acceptable >= 1 usable") Table 10 currently distinguished F. Implementing U require extending API take separate vector usable--- acceptable maneuvers; zero acceptable values, function returns F unconditionally.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: Initial public release. Baseline feature set implementing routine pulmonary function test interpretation ERS/ATS 2022 technical standard, GLI-family reference equations (GLI-2012 GLI Global 2022 spirometry, GLI-2021 static lung volumes, GLI-2017 diffusion capacity 2020 author correction), ERS/ATS 2022 pattern classifier, severity grading, bronchodilator response, PRISm identification, hemoglobin correction, FEV1Q survival index, GOLD 1-4 airflow-limitation grading, Graham 2019 -F spirometry acceptability grading. Pellegrino et al. 2005 legacy primitives retained cross-standard reclassification analyses. Detailed change log development history follows.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: default year argument pft_spirometry(), pft_interpret(), pft_classify(), pft_prism(), pft_volume_subpattern() now 2022 (GLI Global / race-neutral equations) — previously 2012. aligns package current ERS/ATS 2022 technical-standard recommendation removes need race column callers omit year. combined effect always-suffixed output convention pft_spirometry(d) now emits fev1_pred_2022 (fev1_pred), pft_classify(d) looks fev1_lln_2022 default (fev1_lln). reproduce previous behaviour pass year = 2012 explicitly include race column. Spirometry reference outputs pft_spirometry() / pft_interpret() now always carry four-digit GLI-year suffix: fev1_pred_2012, fev1_lln_2012, fev1_zscore_2012, … GLI 2012, existing _2022 columns GLI 2022. Previously default emitted unsuffixed columns 2022 carried suffix; asymmetry meant flipping default future silently change column names code written unsuffixed convention. always-suffix scheme makes source equation explicit every column removes implicit “unsuffixed == default” rule. Lung-volume (Hall 2021) diffusion (GLI 2017) outputs stay unsuffixed now — module currently ships single standard. suffixing convention adopted competing standards emerge. Knock-changes: pft_classify(), pft_prism(), pft_volume_subpattern() gain year argument (default 2012) suffixes spirometry-derived _lln defaults. Standalone callers pass year matching upstream reference-function call. pft_interpret() threads year internal pipeline helpers receive automatically. pft_long()’s year column now populated every spirometry row; volume / diffusion rows remain year = NA modules adopt convention. print.pft_result annotates per-measure rows chosen GLI year (e.g. “FEV1 (2012)”). _2012 _2022 columns present, highest year rendered. pft_plot() deduplicates per measure years present, picking highest year single lollipop renders one z-score per measure. Tests, vignettes, rendered docs updated new convention. wrote code fev1_pred / fev1_zscore / etc., add _2012 suffix names (pass column-name overrides helpers). pft_compare(), print.pft_compare(), summary.pft_compare(), pft_plot(type = "compare") mode removed. GLI 2012 vs GLI Global 2022 reclassification analysis can still reproduced calling pft_interpret(data, year = 2012) pft_interpret(data, year = 2022) computing deltas resulting columns directly. Removed outside package’s core ATS/ERS reference-value + interpretation mission. pft_plot() simplified single-patient lollipop figure; histogram, trajectory, bdr modes removed. signature now pft_plot(data) type argument. Cohort longitudinal figures easier build directly pft_long() output piped ggplot2. pft_cohort_summary() removed. outputs (per-measure z-score quantiles, ATS pattern frequencies, PRISm prevalence, diffusion-category frequencies) easier expressed plain dplyr::group_by() |> summarise() calls pft_interpret() result, optionally piped pft_long() first. pft_dlco_hb_correct() longer inspects hemoglobin argument likely-g/dL inputs (previous behaviour warn multiply 10 value 30). Unit detection scope package; pass g/L directly. Callers relying auto-conversion now get numerically wrong corrections instead warning, audit upstream code produced argument. pft_required_columns() removed. returned hardcoded list column names per function — documentation written code, hand-synced actual function signatures. content now vignette("input-format"). pft_validate() removed. checks (sex coding, age height range, race level membership) duplicated warnings already emitted reference functions’ input normalisation, rest (positive-value, FEV1 ≤ FVC) surface naturally NaN z-scores LMS power transform. Callers can write checks inline cohort still want . pft_glance() broom::glance() S3 method pft_result removed. function returned four passthrough columns plus three trivial row-stats (worst_zscore, n_below_lln, n_above_uln), easier computed inline pft_long() result. pft_long() broom::tidy() S3 method kept. pft_interpret() longer auto-derives fev1fvc_measured fev1_measured / fvc_measured (analogous frc_tlc_measured). Trust--caller: supply ratio column explicitly want pattern classification, PRISm, volume sub-pattern stages run. Note ATS/ERS “best test” workflows reported ratio comes single maneuver may equal fev1 / fvc constructed best--N picks, caller’s explicit ratio safer source.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: pft_classify(), pft_prism(), pft_volume_subpattern(), pft_diffusion_interpret() now accept tidy-eval column-name overrides (bare name, string, !!var) every input column, matching pattern already used pft_spirometry(), pft_volumes(), pft_diffusion(), pft_interpret(). Defaults canonical pft column names, callers using convention pass extra arguments. error message required column absent now reads "required column(s) missing input: ..." (function-specific wording). pft_diffusion_interpret() gains SI.units argument matching pft_diffusion() pft_interpret(). previous silent traditional-vs-SI auto-detect dropped; SI-units callers must now pass SI.units = TRUE (override dlco / kco directly). Threaded pft_interpret() package-level SI.units flag now reaches every stage needs . pft_classify() now tolerates absence tlc / tlc_lln columns: either missing data, existing spirometry-fallback (Stanojevic 2022 Table 5 / Pellegrino 2005 Fig 2) triggers without raising. Previously caller working spirometry-data add explicit NA TLC columns first. pft_long() pivots pft_result long form (one row per (patient, measure)). tidy.pft_result() S3 method dispatches broom installed. pft_diffusion_interpret(data) assigns Hughes & Pride 2012 clinical category (Normal / Parenchymal / Volume loss / Mixed / Vascular / Elevated KCO / ) DLCO, VA, KCO z-scores. Run automatically pft_interpret() diffusion z-scores present. pft_volume_subpattern(data) differentiates six Stanojevic 2022 Figure 10 lung-volume sub-patterns (Normal lung volumes / Large lungs / Hyperinflation / Simple restriction / Complex restriction / Mixed disorder). Auto-run pft_interpret() requisite ratio columns present. pft_fev1q(fev1, sex, age) implements FEV1Q adult mortality index Stanojevic 2022 Box 3. pft_dlco_hb_correct(dlco, hemoglobin, sex, age) applies Cotes 1972 / Stanojevic 2017 hemoglobin correction. Reference Hb 146 g/L (males ≥ 15) 134 g/L (females, males < 15). Hb input must g/L; function detect convert g/dL (see breaking change ).

title: pft 1.0.0
text: pft_quality() — child age cutoff corrected age < 6 age <= 6 per Graham 2019 Table 10; 6-year-old now graded child. pft_quality() — child 10%--highest repeatability rule (Graham 2019 Table 10 footnote) applied; now max(absolute, 0.10 * max(values)) age <= 6. pft_quality() — sessions n >= 2 acceptable maneuvers best-two diff /C/D thresholds graded F; now correctly graded E (“usable poor repeatability”). Grade U (“0 acceptable ≥ 1 usable”) implemented function takes acceptable maneuvers. pft_gold() — added optional fev1fvc argument enforcing GOLD “FEV1/FVC < 0.7” prerequisite (Figure 2.10 header). Default preserves prior behaviour existing callers; passing fev1fvc_measured returns NA non-obstructed rows instead spurious GOLD grade.

New “Input data format” vignette walks data-frame contract, units types per column, override syntax, common errors.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: Bug fix (silent-wrong-sex): sex value "M" previously treated "F" without warning, cohort "Male" / "Female" / "male" etc. silently produced female predictions. pft_spirometry(), pft_volumes(), pft_diffusion() now soft-correct common variants ("male" -> "M", "Female" -> "F", etc.) warning; truly unrecognised values (e.g. "X", "Unknown") set NA rather mis-coded. race values similarly soft-corrected case-insensitively whitespace synonym tolerance ("caucasian" -> "Caucasian", "white" -> "Caucasian", "black" -> "AfrAm", etc.). normalisation findings roll single consolidated warning per call. Missing sex, age, height columns (race GLI 2012) now error clear message listing expected names, rather silently producing -NA output.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: internal reference data now built reproducibly official ERS / ATS source documents. data-raw/build_gli_2012.R, build_gli_2022.R, build_gli_2021_volumes.R, build_gli_2017_diffusion.R read published lookup-table workbook (, unavailable, equation table article PDF) regenerate corresponding .RData CSV artifacts. R/spirometry.R, R/lung_volumes.R, R/diffusion_capacity.R, R/ats_classification.R now carry @references source papers (2020 author correction diffusion equations). Removed data-raw/coeffs_spline_spiro.RData: orphan blob holding GLI 2012 polynomial coefficients ages 25-95, package never used (lookup-table approach R/spirometry.R interpolates directly spline values instead).

title: pft 1.0.0
text: pft_quality(values, age) grades set acceptable spirometry maneuvers -F per Graham et al. ATS/ERS 2019 spirometry standardization update (doi:10.1164/rccm.201908-1590ST). Tighter repeatability thresholds applied children 6. pft_gold(fev1_pctpred) returns GOLD COPD severity grade (1-4) FEV1 % predicted.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: pft_severity(zscore) returns one "normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe" per Stanojevic et al. ERJ 2022 z-score cut points (>= -1.645, > -2.5, > -4, <= -4). pft_bdr(pre, post, predicted) classifies BDR per 2022 criterion (>10% change relative predicted, replacing earlier 12%/200 mL rule). pft_prism(data) adds prism logical column flagging Preserved Ratio Impaired Spirometry (FEV1 LLN, FEV1/FVC LLN). Requires spirometry; need TLC. pft_change(z1, z2, r) computes conditional change z-score recommended Stanojevic 2022 interpreting serial PFT measurements time. Configurable autocorrelation r.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: pft_interpret(data) single-call workflow auto-detects inputs present emits complete Stanojevic 2022-compliant interpretation: reference values, z-scores, percent predicted, severity grading, ATS pattern, PRISm flag, bronchodilator response. recommended entry point clinical-style reporting. pft_plot(result) generates clinical-style z-score lollipop plot severity-band shading. Requires ggplot2 (Suggests).

what: New outputs: z-score and percent predicted
title: pft 1.0.0
text: pft_spirometry(), pft_volumes(), pft_diffusion() now optionally compute z-scores percent predicted. Supply <measure>_measured column input data frame (e.g. fev1_measured, frc_measured, dlco_measured) function appends <measure>_zscore <measure>_pctpred columns alongside existing <measure>_pred / <measure>_lln / <measure>_uln. Backwards compatible: callers supply demographics continue get three existing reference-value columns nothing else. z-score uses LMS formula ((measured/M)^L - 1) / (L*S); percent predicted (measured / M) * 100. propagate NA measured value, LMS parameters, LLN expected. Z-score formula sanity (z = 0 predicted, ~+/-1.645 LLN/ULN) tested every measure across three functions. GLI 2022 oracle CSV tests/testthat/gli_2022_oracle.csv covers z-score percent predicted well predicted LLN, validated tolerance 1e-8.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: Bug fix: pft_spirometry(), pft_volumes(), pft_diffusion() previously crashed “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed” error row missing value (NA) sex, age, height. now skip rows emit NA reference values row, matching behaviour already provided missing race (spirometry) pft_classify(). Real clinical PFT data routinely contains missing demographics; prior behaviour required callers filter NAs .

title: pft 1.0.0
text: ANNN (isolated low FEV1) → “Normal” NANN (isolated low FVC) → “Non-specific” Previously two swapped. change re-labels patients whose spirometry profile “isolated low FEV1 + everything else normal” (previously “Non-specific”, now “Normal”) vice versa isolated low FVC. See notes/ats_classification_label_fix.md clinical-review memo. Bug fix: “normal” branch comparing FVC fev1_lln instead fvc_lln. Combined label-swap , patients FVC slightly FVC LLN FEV1 LLN now correctly routed “Non-specific” rather silently labelled “Normal”. classifier expanded 16 combinations (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC, TLC) statuses, adding new ats_pattern_combination output column. (Initial expansion: previous internal release; bug fixes: release.)

title: pft 1.0.0
text: Test coverage grew 36 101 tests across four test files. Additions include predicted-value (median) comparisons GLI web calculator ground truth, NA-propagation tests, --range tests, structural / column-contract tests, clinical-scenario suite ats_classification grounded Stanojevic 2022 Figure 8 / Table 5 / Table 8. New GLI 2022 / “GLI Global” oracle: frozen 30-row ground-truth fixture tests/testthat/gli_2022_oracle.csv covering predicted, LLN, z-score, percent predicted FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC. Regenerated via data-raw/build_gli_2022_oracle.R (see script provenance). static CSV ships; test-time dependency external package.

title: pft 1.0.0
text: New README installation, usage, citations, research-use disclaimer. New inst/CITATION citation("pft") returns package underlying reference papers bibentry objects. MIT license file now correctly declared DESCRIPTION shipped via LICENSE / LICENSE.md. notes/ats_classification_label_fix.md records rationale clinical-review questions ATS pattern-label changes .

title: pft 1.0.0
text: Project now uses renv dependency management. Reference paper PDFs supplement workbooks live papers/ excluded git R CMD build tarball (copyrighted publisher content). notes/ (clinical-review memos similar) also Rbuildignored. docs/ reserved pkgdown-built site (gitignored; built deployed gh-pages branch .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml).
